pageCaption.value: Determined to protect the remnants of indigenous forests, while at the same time improving living standards for rural people, Maria Eliza Manteca Oñate has established a nature reserve near her home village in the north of Ecuador. She has also launched a successful education programme, whereby adults and children learn sustainable farming techniques at a model farm.

She has also launched a successful education programme, whereby adults and children learn sustainable farming techniques at a model farm.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Naturalist and professional diver Sandra Bessudo has worked for decades to conserve marine life around Malpelo Island, off the coast of Colombia.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

metadescription.value: As the Malpelo Island Hope Spot Champion, diver Sandra Bessudo leads expeditions to monitor the region twice a year. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Mission Blue: Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Hunters and fishermen almost eradicated Atlantic puffins from Maine’s offshore islands in the 19th century. The innovative techniques that 1987 Rolex Laureate Stephen W. Kress used to bring them back have inspired major changes in seabird conservation.

metadescription.value: Hunters and fishermen almost eradicated Atlantic puffins from Maine’s offshore islands in the 19th century. The innovative techniques that 1987 Rolex Laureate Stephen W. Kress used to bring them back have inspired major changes in seabird conservation.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards for Enterprise - Latest Laureates - Video

pageCaption.value: As part of its Perpetual Planet Initiative, Rolex is pleased to announce the new Laureates of the Rolex Awards for Enterprise.

metadescription.value: Discover the 2023 Rolex Awards Laureates, five pioneers striving towards a perpetual planet through ambitious projects. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: The Canadian-American palaeontologist, Elizabeth Nicholls, overcame numerous obstacles to extract the fossilised remains of a 220-million-year-old, giant marine reptile in a remote region of Canada.

metadescription.value: The Canadian-American palaeontologist, Elizabeth Nicholls, overcame numerous obstacles to extract the fossilised remains of a 220-million-year-old, giant marine reptile in a remote region of Canada.

pageTitle.value: Rolex.org - David Schweidenback gives bicycles new life

pageCaption.value: By giving new life to used bicycles, David Schweidenback, who won a Rolex Award in 2000, is improving the lives of people in many developing countries. Pedals for Progress, the organisation he founded in 1991, has collected and exported more than 159,000 bikes – and has extended its activities to sewing-machines and new spare parts.

WaPageTitle.value: Bicycle ride to a new life? - Video

metadescription.value: By recycling discarded bicycles, David Schweidenback is giving people the means to pursue employment, commerce and education. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Bicycle ride to a new life? - Video

pageTitle.value: Vital role for ancient medicine - Video

pageCaption.value: Tibetan medicine has been practised for more than 1,000 years in Ladakh, in India’s far north. The very survival of this complex system of healing, ritual and belief — known as Amchi medicine — was threatened by the 20th century’s huge social changes. But over the past six years French anthropologist and ethno-pharmacologist Laurent Pordié has led a campaign that will help ensure Ladakhis continue to benefit from Amchi medicine for generations to come.

WaPageTitle.value: Vital role for ancient medicine - Video
